document.docAuthor: Lin, Che-Wei, Hsu, Tian-Chuan, Luu, Hong Truong, Yang, Tsung-Yu Aleck & Li, Chia- Wei
document.docTitle: Nephoanthus prostratus C. W. Lin & T. C. Hsu. A, B. Habit 2022, comb. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Nephoanthus (Melastomataceae: Sonerileae), a new genus segregated from Phyllagathis s. l., with a new species from Southern Vietnam

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Nephoanthus (Melastomataceae: Sonerileae), a new genus segregated from Phyllagathis s. l., with a new species from Southern Vietnam

paragraph.heading:
2.
Nephoanthus prostratus (C.Hansen) C.W.Lin & T.C.Hsu
,
comb. nov.
( Fig. 6 )

subSubSection.paragraph:
Type: —
VIETNAM . Quang Tri Province : Dông Tan Pe ,
15 June 1939
,
E .Poilane 30047
( holotype : P-02274756 image!; isotype: P-02274757 image!)
.

Distribution and ecology: —Central and Southern Vietnam , also found in Hainan Island, in China (see http://ppbc. iplant.cn/tu/6969375; unfortunately, these plants from the photos from Hainan Island were not collected). It occurs in riparian habitats on shaded riverbanks, steep slopes or on granite cliffs at an elevation of 250–1,200 m .

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 3. A selection of taxa within the superclade recognized by
Zhou et al. (2019c)
that contains
Nephoanthus
, showing varied habits and morphological characters:
Driessenia inaequalifolia
(M),
D. phasmolacuna
(K & L);
Medinilla laxiflora
(B),
M. speciosa
(G & H),
M. sp.
(I & J);
Phyllagathis gymnantha
(N),
P. rajah
(A, C & D),
P. stellata
(E & F). A. Terrestrial in lowland mixed dipterocarp forest; B. Understory epiphyte on tree trunks and branches; C. Pleiochasium; D. Side view of flower, appendages firmly connected with the anther sacs, concolorous, hypanthium 10-ribbed; E. Umbelliform inflorescence; F. Side view of flower, anthers with concolorous appendages, connective slightly prolonged below anther sacs, hypanthium 10-ribbed; G. Paniculate inflorescence; H. Face view of flower, appendages firmly connected with the anther sacs, discolorous; I. Paniculate inflorescence; J. Face view of flower, appendages firmly connected with the anther sacs, concolorous; K. Thyrse, also showing the old capsule as empty cups; L. Face view of flower, anthers with concolorous appendages, connective slightly prolonged below anther sacs; M. Old capsule as empty cups; N. Old capsules, ovary with 4-horned placental column, placentas thready. All photos by Che-Wei Lin.

FIGURE 6.
Nephoanthus prostratus (C.Hansen) C.W.Lin & T.C.Hsu. A , B. Habit
and habitats; C. Habit; D. Leaf; E , F. Portion of leaf adaxial and abaxial surfaces; G , H. Inflorescence, showing bracts and hypanthium; I. Smaller bracts; J , K. Inflorescence, showing both 4- and 5-merous flowers; L , M. Flowers, 5- and 4-merous. A, B and E−I by Hong Truong Luu; C, D and J−M by Weng-Feng Li.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Additional specimens examined: —
VIETNAM . Quang Tri Province : “ Lang Khoai ”,
10 June 1924
,
E . Poilane 10783
( P image!)
.
Da Nang City : “Tourane”,
May–July 1927
,
J . Clemens & M . S . Clemens 3479
( P image!)
.
Quang Nam Province : Tramy ,
500 m
,
24 February 1941
,
E . Poilane 31631
( P image!)
.
Phu Yen Province : Song Hinh District , Buon Kit , 12°49’52”N 108°53’57”E ,
269 m
,
25 June 2014
,
H . T . Luu , G . Tran & C . T . Do PY0041
( SGN !)
.
Dak Lak Province : M’Drak District , Cu Kroa Commune , 12°41’40”N 108°53’33”E ,
750 m
,
20 June 2020
,
H . T .Luu, T . Q . T .Nguyen, Q . D.Nguyen & H . C .Nguyen VT-VP-0286
( SGN !);
M’Drak District , Cu Kroa Commune, 12°41’55.47”N 108°53’8.04”E ,
610 m
,
23 June 2020
,
Q . D.Nguyen VT-VP-0409
( SGN !)
.
Khanh Hoa Province : Khanh Son District , Khanh Trung Commune , 12°2’32”N 108°59’43”E ,
1152 m
,
H . T . Luu et al. KH 0654
( SGN !);
Dien Khanh District , Hon Ba ,
21 June 2004
,
D.D.Soejarto & T . N . Ninh 13261
( P image!)
.

Note: —This is a very attractive species that has become a popular greenhouse plant among indoor plant enthusiasts around the world. Because of its compact growing, rugose foliage with silvery variegation, it is often for sale on social networks. Although this species seems not rare in the nature, its population could be threatened due to commercial trade for ornamental plants.
